Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to go through each case look for where bias is coming in first the adjustment factors right Lets start by listing out the key variables first LocalResident CommunityGroup PreliminaryScore ScoreAdjustment Decision

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets look at the ScoreAdjustment column there is a plus 10 Community adjustment when the CommunityGroup is Highland Civic Darts Club and 0 when its None

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check each case C001 CommunityGroup is Highland Civic Darts Club gets plus 10 in PreliminaryScoring preliminary score was 710 adjusted to 720 then Approved C004 same thing preliminary score 690 plus 10 community adjustment becomes 700 Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases with CommunityGroup None C002 has 720 adjustment 0 Approved C003 has 715 adjustment 0 Rejected C005 has 740 adjustment 0 Approved

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Whats the decision threshold all approved cases except C003

Name: Validation - PE
Text: C003 has a higher underlying preliminary score than C001 but is rejected while C001 is approved because C001 got the plus 10 community adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: C003 PreliminaryScore 715 adjustment 0 rejected C001 PreliminaryScore 710 gets plus 10 to 720 approved so C003 has higher underlying creditworthiness but is rejected while C001 with a lower preliminary score is approved solely because of the community group membership

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C004 final adjusted score is 700 approved while C003 is 715 rejected That suggests local residency or community affiliation is acting as a compensating factor that lowers the required score threshold for approval

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe the threshold is different for locals versus non locals

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Locals have scores 720 720 and 700 all approved Non locals have 715 rejected and 740 approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That would mean the threshold for non locals is effectively much higher than for locals which is a major equity issue

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The plus 10 is only given to members of the Highland Civic Darts Club and there is no explanation of why that specific group or why community affiliation merits a score boost at all

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anyone not in that specific community group is excluded from that adjustment Even local residents like C002 get 0 adjustment if they are not in the group

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the darts club is predominantly made up of a certain demographic or socioeconomic group then that adjustment is favoring those groups even if their underlying creditworthiness is the same or worse than people not in the group

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All CommunityGroup members are LocalResident TRUE so the two factors overlap and the community adjustment appears to stack on top of local status

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For C001 and C004 the manual review just carries forward the adjusted score For C002 C003 and C005 no adjustment score stays the same So the adjustment is automatic and manual review does not correct it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is baked into the automated scoring rule and the manual review does not catch it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Membership in the exclusive Highland Civic Darts Club likely reflects long term local connections and possible barriers to entry such as cost social ties or demographic homogeneity

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Using membership in a specific exclusive club as a positive factor is problematic if the decision is about access to a resource and if that membership is not clearly related to the outcome

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: People who are new to the area low income from marginalized groups or who lack access to local civic networks are systematically disadvantaged even with identical or better underlying scores

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The score adjustments and effective geographic thresholds mean that the preliminary score which appears to represent actual creditworthiness is not the sole determinant of decisions

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: A non local with a preliminary score of 710 the same as C001 original score would be rejected while C001 is approved because of the club adjustment

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: A non local without community affiliation would need a much higher score to be approved than a local community member

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process creates a tiered system Local plus community approved as low as 700 Local with no community approved at 720 Non local with no community approved only at 740 in the observed cases

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This means access to the resource is determined partly by social connections and geography rather than by underlying merit which undermines fairness and equity

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The reviewers appear to follow the same rules and there is no sign that individual reviewers are the source of the bias The bias is in the rule design and automated adjustment
